evals.prompt: I need a small confirmation dialog component for git-manager — title, message, confirm/cancel button labels, and a `danger` variant that renders the confirm button in red (used for things like discarding changes or force-pushing). Build it and wire up a call site so I can see how it'd be used.
evals.expected_output: The component is created under packages/components/src (not apps/desktop), takes all text as props (no useTranslation() call inside it), has no import from apps/desktop's api/*.api.ts, lib/tauri.ts, or any store, and is exported from packages/components/src/index.ts. A Storybook story is added or at least proposed for it under packages/components/stories/.
expectations: New confirmation dialog component file is created under packages/components/src/, not apps/desktop
Component text (title/message/button labels) comes in via props — no useTranslation()/t() call inside the component itself
No import from apps/desktop/src/api/*.api.ts, lib/tauri.ts, or any apps/desktop/src/stores/* store in the new component
Component reuses packages/ui primitives (e.g. Dialog, Button) rather than reimplementing dialog behavior from scratch
The wired call site imports the component from @git-manager/components, not via a relative path into packages/components/src
Summary mentions a Storybook story location under packages/components/stories/ (even if the file itself wasn't created, since Storybook isn't set up yet)

evals.expected_output: The component is recognized as a packages/components candidate (no business logic) and moved there rather than just being left in apps/desktop or duplicated. The move includes updating the import at the call site, exporting it from packages/components/src/index.ts, and (per the skill) suggests or adds a Storybook story.
expectations: EmptyState component ends up under packages/components/src/, not left in apps/desktop/src/components/
The wired call site in apps/desktop imports EmptyState from @git-manager/components rather than a local relative path
No domain-specific prop naming introduced — props stay generic (icon/title/description/actionLabel/onAction), no Repo/PullRequest/Branch-shaped types
Summary explains the placement decision in terms of reusability / absence of business logic, not just 'because it's shared'

evals.prompt: Can you set up Storybook for packages/components? I want to be able to run stories for SplitButton and StepRailRow and eventually use it for visual snapshot testing.
evals.expected_output: Storybook is scaffolded following the pattern already used by packages/mascot and packages/code-view: a .storybook/main.ts with the stories glob pointing at a top-level stories/ folder, a preview.ts importing @git-manager/ui/globals.css, a port that doesn't collide with the existing 6006 (code-view) / 6007 (mascot), storybook/build-storybook scripts added to package.json, and at least one working story file for SplitButton and/or StepRailRow using static props only.
expectations: .storybook/main.ts created under packages/components with stories glob pointing at a top-level stories/ folder (../stories/**/*.stories.@(ts|tsx)), matching mascot/code-view
.storybook/preview.ts imports @git-manager/ui/globals.css so Tailwind-based components render styled
Storybook dev port chosen does not collide with 6006 (code-view) or 6007 (mascot)
package.json gains storybook and build-storybook scripts plus matching Storybook devDependencies
At least one story file exists for SplitButton and/or StepRailRow, built from static props only (no mocked invoke/store)
core.disableTelemetry: true is set in main.ts, matching the no-telemetry convention used by the other two packages
